name: San Francisco-Style Cioppino
recipeIngredient: 28 oz Can Crushed Tomatoes with Puree
1 lb White Fish (cut into 1/2 x 2-inch strips)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a Dutch oven or large heavy pan over medium heat.
2. Add onions and sauté 1 minute without browning.
3. Add carrot, green pepper, leek, celery, and fennel; sauté 5 minutes.
4. Stir in crushed tomatoes with puree, tomato paste, water, salt, pepper, basil, oregano, thyme, bay leaves, and cayenne pepper.
5. Partially cover and simmer over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally. (The sauce can be covered and kept warm over low heat for several hours longer, stirring occasionally.)
6. Remove bay leaves.
7. Heat remaining 1/4 cup oil in a large skillet over high heat.
8. Add garlic and cook 5 seconds.
9. Immediately add fish, shrimp, and scallops; sauté until just cooked through, about 2-4 minutes.
10. Add seafood to the sauce and stir gently.
11. Pour wine into the skillet and cook 30 seconds over medium heat, stirring up any loose bits.
12. Add clams to the skillet. Cover and cook until clams open, 2-6 minutes. Transfer opened clams to the sauce; discard any that do not open.
13. Gently stir shrimp and crab meat into the sauce.
14. Cover and cook cioppino until all seafood is hot, about 2-3 more minutes.
15. Serve cioppino in large casserole dishes or shallow soup plates, garnished with chopped Italian Parsley.
